UNCLASSIFIED.content: Prepared by: Alexis Schewebach Date: 1/12/2026 – V1.6 Cegeka reference: Managed SD-WAN CONFIDENTIAL Service Description Cegeka Managed SD-WAN powered by Cisco Meraki Table of Contents Definition	3 1	Service summary	4 1.1	Services	4 1.1.1	Services Components	4 1.2	General design consideration for the SD-WAN Network	7 1.2.1	SD-WAN Sites supported design.	9 1.2.2	SD-WAN Service license	14 1.2.3	SD-WAN Assurance and Reporting platform	14 1.3	Enabling Service	16 1.3.1	Enabling Service Overview	16 1.3.2	Service Request Summary	20 1.3.3	Out of scope	21 2	Service Levels Management	22 Proactive Monitoring - Incident Prioritization	23 2.1	Per Topology SLA definition	25 3	Service Prerequisites & Conditions	30 3.1	Hardware and Licenses	30 3.1.1	SD-WAN Security Gateway (Physical/Hardware)	30 3.1.2	Licenses and/or subscriptions Managed SD-WAN	30 3.1.3	Customer owned environment licenses and/or subscriptions	31 3.2	Contract Duration	31 3.3	Terms and Conditions	31 3.4	Technical Implementation Pre-Requisites	32

Definition.content: In this section an overview will be given of the used terms and definition specifically of this product. “8x5” “ Business Hours” : Availability of the support services Monday through Friday from 8:00 to 18:00 Local Time (excluding public local holidays). “12x5”: Availability of the support services Monday through Friday from 8:00 to 20:00 local time excluding public local holidays. “12x6”: Availability of the support services Monday through Saturday from 8:00 to 20:00 local time excluding public local holidays. “24x7”: Availability of the support services 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, including weekends and public holidays. “Best Effort” : all the required activities will be done to address a request/issue without a specific time guarantee. “TTR” , “Time to Repair”: refers to the amount of time it takes to resolve an incident with a Px priority. "Contract Date”: The date that the last party signed the contract. “Horizon”: Managed Service Portal provided by Cegeka for tickets tracking, reporting and change management. “Managed Device” : is network which can be a device, appliance, software application or a system installed on the Customer’s premises or Cegeka’s Datacentre which is monitored and/or managed by Cegeka as part of this Service. “Cegeka Network Advisor”: Network expert assigned to the Customer who will deliver non-technical support. “Network Operations Center” or “NOC” : The organization within Cegeka that operates the Managed service including Service desk. “NOC Platform” combination of systems needed by Cegeka to perform the Service. “Time and material” or “T&M” : work in which the Customer agrees to pay Cegeka based upon the time spent by Cegeka to perform the work. “Overlay” network: virtual network created on top of an existing physical network. “Underlay” network is the physical network infrastructure.  It consists of physical hardware devices such as routers, switches, cables and networking equipment generally provided by a Telecom Operator. The underlay network provides the basic connectivity and transport to create SD-WAN “Overlay” Network. Physical infrastructure can be XDSL, XPON, Fiber access line. Mobile transport network like 4G and 5G are also considered as underlay. SD-WAN Central Management Platform: The SD-WAN Central Management Platform also called “Meraki Dashboard” is a centralized system used to orchestrate, control and monitor the Meraki SD-WAN components. SD-WAN Security gateway Physical: The SD-WAN security gateway Physical device is a physical networking equipment located at the edge of the network. The role of the device is to connect the local area network of a location to the Wide Area Network. SD-WAN Security gateway Virtual: The SD-WAN security gateway Virtual is a Virtual networking equipment located into the cloud infrastructure of a cloud provider like Azure, Amazon Web Services or Google Cloud. The role of the device is to integrate the cloud infrastructure of the cloud service provider into the SD-WAN network of the customer. WAN Provider: A company that delivers wide-area network connectivity by supplying the underlay network circuits (such as MPLS, leased lines, fiber, or broadband) used to interconnect geographically dispersed sites via the Managed SD-WAN.

Service summary.content: Software Defined Wide Area Network (SD-WAN) is a networking technology concept that enables the efficient deployment of a wide area network connecting branch offices, remote sites, and multi-cloud environments. By centralizing control and introducing an abstraction layer that separates the physical network, known as the underlay, from the data transport layer, known as the overlay, SD-WAN enables a more efficient and dynamic network management. This approach supports access diversity, intelligent routing, and advanced visibility, which are essential requirements for modern networks. Cegeka offers comprehensive managed SD-WAN services that span the entire lifecycle of an infrastructure and component-based Software-Defined WAN network. These services encompass design, configuration, installation, management, monitoring, maintenance, and performance management as necessary. Cegeka’s Managed SD-WAN service liberates customers from the day-to-day management of SD-WAN networks. Depending on the selected service level, this may include the management of underlay providers and advanced advisory services for capacity and performance management. Cegeka remotely manages the SD-WAN network from its operation center, proactively addressing faults and ensuring the service’s continuous operation. Furthermore, Cegeka provides reports on the status of customers’ WAN networks and promptly informs them of any potential incidents.

Services Components.content: The managed SD-WAN service relies on the combination of The SD-WAN Central Management Platform also called “Meraki Dashboard” is a centralized system used to orchestrate, control and monitor the SD-WAN Meraki components. The SD-WAN centralized system for the Managed SD-WAN service powered by Cisco Meraki relies on a cloud-based solution hosted by Cisco. Cisco Meraki data centers are located around the world, enabling high-availability local data containment for data sovereignty in sensitive countries and regions, and high-speed connections to facilitate reliable cloud management communication. These data centers hold certifications such as PCI, SAS70 Type II/SSAE, PCI, and ISO27001 Each region (North and South America, Europe, Asia, China, Canada) has, at minimum, a geographically matched pair (for failover) of data centers where any endpoint’s primary Meraki server will be located. Upon Network creation, Cegeka can select which region where data are hosted in. The default region is Europe. The Europe data centers are in Germany. For customers with globally dispersed networks, separate organizations may be created for each data storage region. Available regions are: North America, South America, Asia-Pacific, Canada, India, China and Europe (service default). The Central management platform assures a segregation of the network and management data. For SD-WAN security gateway devices to communicate with the Meraki Cloud, Meraki leverages a proprietary lightweight encrypted tunnel using AES-256 encryption while management data is in transit. Within the tunnel itself, Meraki leverages HTTPS and protocol buffers for a secure and efficient solution. The system separates management data from user data. User data (network traffic, web browsing, internal applications, etc.) does not flow through the Cisco Meraki cloud and instead flows directly to the destination across the WAN. The SD-WAN Central Management System will be configured by Cegeka: The SD-WAN Security gateway is a physical or virtual networking equipment located at the edge of the network. The role of the device is to connect the local area network of a location to the Wide Area Network. The SD-WAN Security gateway Physical is connected to the CPE(s) of the WAN provider(s) with a maximum of 2 circuits per site (excluding mobile backup).  The device processes the traffic based on the configuration pushed by the Central Management platform. The SD-WAN Security gateway will be configured to support the functions below. Some restrictions may apply according to the chosen license level (essential/advantage), service level and added value services: The list of supported SD-WAN Security gateway physical devices is available as a technical annex “Technical Annex managed SD-WAN Security gateway: list of supported devices” Mobile Backup Solution The mobile backup solution is deployed using a cellular gateway that provides backup cellular connectivity when connected to the SD-WAN Security gateway. The cellular gateway converts 4G or 5G signals into Ethernet, ensuring reliable internet access for critical sites. The list of supported equipment is available as a technical annex “Technical Annex managed SD-WAN Security gateway: list of supported devices” The SD-WAN Security gateway Virtual extends the SD-WAN network to hybrid and multi-cloud environments. The Virtual device can be deployed in Azure, AWS and Google cloud, providing automated site-to-site connectivity. The list of supported SD-WAN Security gateway virtual devices is available as a technical annex “Technical Annex managed SD-WAN Security gateway: list of supported devices”

General design consideration for the SD-.header: General design consideration for the SD-WAN Network
General design consideration for the SD-.content: As part of service provisioning, Cegeka supports a series of standard designs to meet the common needs of an SD-WAN network. We’ll consider underlay and overlay network designs. The physical infrastructure (underlay) on which the SD-WAN underlay is built can take various forms. In the illustration provided, we assume an internet-only setup.  By using, for Underlay architecture , the internet as a standard solution, we can reduce costs but still maintain a great form of stability. SD-WAN offers Carrier Neutrality allowing us to select the best solution per location. To accommodate internet connectivity, any of the following circuit technologies offered by a carrier provider are supported: As a standard, the Managed SD-WAN Service does not encompass the responsibility for ordering, provisioning, or maintaining the underlying infrastructure. Consequently, the customer is solely accountable for contracting with one or multiple providers to establish the necessary circuits for deploying the Managed SD-WAN solutions. As an alternative, Cegeka offers Managed WAN infrastructure through a dedicated “Cegeka Managed WAN” service contract.  The SD-WAN equipment will be connected to the carrier/underlay network provider's CPE either via an RJ45 copper connection or via a fiber link connected to an SFP/SFP+. From an Overlay architecture perspective, where the tunnels connecting all the network site are defined, full mesh and hub & spoke topologies are supported. Because these are persistent VPN tunnels, the hub and spoke architecture is being used as standard design for cost and performance reasons.  However, SD-WAN is also possible on top of an existing MPLS WAN solution (Multiprotocol Label Switching Wide Area Network). MPLS networks are private wide area networks operated by wan providers that offer great stability and hence are more expensive than the publicly available internet. An MPLS design will require a specific assessment and is not part of the standard service offer.

SD-WAN Sites supported design..content: Within the service we provide support for the following deployment and redundancy methods. Deployment model Deployment Appliance redundancy Dual circuit active/active Dual circuit active/backup Single device, single circuit Spoke N/A N/A N/A Single device, single circuit, mobile backup Spoke N/A N/A l Single device, dual circuit Spoke N/A l l Single device, dual circuit, mobile backup Spoke N/A l l Dual device, dual circuit Hub/Spoke l l l Azure, single device Hub/Spoke N/A N/A N/A Azure, dual device Hub/Spoke l N/A N/A Any other combinations are considered as non-standard and is not permitted.  In this topology, typically for a small branch site, one SD-WAN security appliance is connected to one single circuit. The LAN can have multiple network segments for which the SD-WAN security appliance will be the gateway. This option is only applicable for spokes.  In this topology, a passive mobile backup option is introduced. This mobile backup option becomes active once the active circuit gets disconnected. The mobile backup solution is deployed using an additional CPE device included in the service. This option requires a valid SIM card with an appropriate (preferrable flat fee) data subscription plan. The SIM card and subscription plan are out of scope of this service. This option is only applicable for spokes.  In this topology, two independent circuits are configured in either an active/active or active/passive setup. When used in active/active the bandwidth and capacity of both circuits can be used by introducing load balancing. Whereas in active/passive, only the capacity of one circuit can be used. This option is only applicable for spokes.  In this topology, two independent circuits are configured in either an active/active or active/passive setup. When used in active/active the bandwidth and capacity of both circuits can be used by introducing load balancing. Whereas in active/passive, only the capacity of one circuit can be used. In addition, a passive mobile backup option is introduced. This mobile backup option becomes active once the active circuits get disconnected. The mobile backup solution is deployed using an additional CPE device included in the service. This option requires a valid SIM card with an appropriate (preferrable flat fee) data subscription plan. The SIM card and subscription plan are out of scope of this service. This option is only applicable for spokes.  This topology introduces full redundancy, both on hardware and on circuit level. Two SD-WAN security appliances are configured in an active/passive cluster. The passive member will become active upon failure of the active member. In addition, two independent circuits are configured in either an active/active or active/passive setup. When used in active/active the bandwidth and capacity of both circuits can be used by introducing load balancing. Whereas in active/passive, only the capacity of one circuit can be used. This option is applicable for spokes and mandatory for hubs.  This topology provides SD-WAN security gateway in Azure. The SD-WAN security gateway in Azure will exclusively handle traffic to and from sites connected via the SD‑WAN. All other traffic originating from cloud VMs or services will be routed through the designated cloud infrastructure. The vMX in Azure can be configured as a HUB or a Spoke. Setup, management, monitoring, and maintenance of the underlaying virtual machine and Azure network infrastructure is not part of this managed service.  This topology provides redundancy for the Azure SD-WAN security gateway. Two SD-WAN security gateways are deployed independently in Azure. They do not synchronize configuration or state information. Both gateways are active, and failover is managed through cloud-specific settings (e.g. load balancer) not part of this managed service. The SD-WAN security gateways in Azure will exclusively handle traffic to and from sites connected via the SD-WAN. All other traffic originating from cloud VMs or services will be routed through the designated cloud infrastructure. The vMX in Azure is typically configured as hub, but spoke deployment is also possible. Setup, management, monitoring, and maintenance of the underlaying virtual machine and Azure network infrastructure (including the load balancer) is not part of this managed service.

SD-WAN Service license.content: The SD-WAN solution offers 2 levels of license to meet different needs. One license is required per active device. Redundant equipment configuration (warm spare) requires only one license. The licenses are linked to a specific family type as showed in the table below: License type Device Family Hardware type MX Small Essential Small MX67, vMX-S* MX Small Advantage MX Medium Essential Medium MX75, MX85, vMX-M MX Medium Advantage MX Large Essential Large MX95, MX105, vMX-L MX Large Advantage MX X-Large Essential X-Large MX250, MX450, vMX-XL MX X-Large Advantage Advantage license is not available for all vMX Virtual Security Gateway Managed service supported features Cegeka shall deploy the service using a defined set of features designed to ensure service performance, stability, and quality for the Essential and Advantage license tier. Cegeka shall not enable or implement any feature that may adversely affect the security, stability, or overall quality of the service. The feature set may be adapted during the contract term to accommodate service evolution and shall follow Cegeka’s established release management process. Any such adaptations shall be implemented in a manner that maintains compliance with agreed service levels and does not compromise performance or security. The list of supported features is made available through the document “Technical Annex: List of Meraki LX Supported Features” Customer acknowledges that feature changes outside the defined release management process, if requested by the Customer, may require additional assessment and approval by Cegeka. Any resulting impact on service performance, compliance, or cost shall be communicated to the Customer prior to implementation.

SD-WAN Assurance and Reporting platform.content: The Assurance and Reporting of Managed SD-WAN Network is delivered through the Cisco Meraki Dashboard. A read access account is provided to the designated contact(s) from the customer with a limit of 5 accounts. The Dashboard allows (not limited to): A global overview of all sites makes it possible to identify very quickly whether sites are affected by a complete or partial malfunction via a color code. VPN Summary view. The VPN Status page provides detailed, useful information about all VPN tunnels on your SD-WAN organization. This page provides real-time status updates between your SD-WAN VPN peers. Status of the underlay access(es) . The status of the single of two active-active or active-passive underlay accesses (load balancing vs. failover) and the optional 4G/5G mobile backup are visible via the dashboard. The consumption of the links is also provided in real time and up to 30 days as well as the quality of these links (latency and loss). Clients view . The Cisco Meraki Dashboard offers the ability to monitor network clients.

Enabling Service Overview.content: Enabling service. The table below summarizes the activities available for the Managed SD-WAN service. Task Description Included Implementaion Coordination, design and configuration tasks defined in the Implenation SOW provided as annex of this service description l Access Management Account and access management by central account repository. l Asset management Keeping up-to-date documentation of the managed systems into Cegeka’s CMDB. l Network diagrams Maintain network diagrams (OSI Layer2 and OSI Layer3) related to the assets deployed in the context of the Managed SD-WAN service. l IP address management (IPAM) IP address allocating and documentation of the attached network topology into Cegeka’s IPAM tooling. l Device recovery plans Creating and maintain the device recovery plan. l License management Monitor the licenses necessary for the network components which are being managed. l Logging Managing the (security) log file generation (via the integrated solution offered by Cisco Meraki). Retention and storage boundary is managed / guarded according to the Cisco Meraki Cloud Data Retention Policies l Supplier contact Contact vendors, service providers, and other 3 rd parties that are being used within the operational field of this managed service. Underlay wan providers are not in scope of this service l Service management Event management Incident management Problem management l Change management All changes related to events/incidents or problems that might prevent Cegeka from assuring device SLAs are included. l Backup and restore (configuration files) Scheduling and taking automated backups of the configuration files from the component to a storage system of Cegeka when technically possible with a retention of 1 month. Restoring the configuration in case of failure. l Software lifecycle management Software updates provide new and improved functionality while addressing existing issues such as security and bugs. The software update schedule and versioning is solely defined by Cegeka. Cegeka will perform an update based on planned work schedule with a minimum of 1 major release per year. l Security patch management Adjacent to software lifecycle management, Cegeka will engage in emergency patch management for critical software vulnerabilities with a high risk of exploitation within the Managed SD-WAN architecture. l On-site repair On-site repair for confirmed broken physical devices within the service region. l Change management Standard changes Day-2 standard operational changes l Fixed fee Change management Non-standard changes Day-2 non-standard operational changes Project SD-WAN Performance management Periodic SD-WAN performance management review Project Periodic Security policy reviews Periodic security policy review Project Service Level Management Proactive Service Level Management by means of a dedicated service manager. Optional : Included /  fixed fee: list of predefined changes associated to a fixed fee /Project: optional service delivered at a pre-agreed price based on a defined scope. The following standard services are included in the SD-WAN Managed service level: Implementation To deliver the services, Cegeka will undertake the following activities during the technical implementation phase: A more detailed description of the activities will be formalized in a “Managed SD-WAN: Statement of Work” document. This document can differ from the described activities above and is leading. Availability management based on the defined SLAs as described in this. Documentation management and Asset management : Cegeka will keep up to date documentation of the managed systems, document IP addresses and provide account and access management in a central account repository. Cegeka will maintain network diagrams (OSI Layer 2 and OSI Layer 3) of the Managed SD-WAN Security gateway devices and central management and analytics components. Specific relevant information will be shared with Customer, including network diagrams via read-only access on select Solution components and/or the Horizon dashboard. Device Recovery plans : Cegeka will Create, maintain, and test device recovery plans of the Managed SD-WAN Security gateways and central management and analytics components. License management : Cegeka will monitor the required licenses of the Managed SD-WAN Security Gateway and central management and analytics components. Logging : Cegeka will provide logging services as part of the predefined SD-WAN architecture. The service is delivered through the Cisco Meraki Platform in accordance with the Cisco Meraki retention policies and service level agreements. Backup & Restore : Cegeka will schedule and take automated backups of the configuration files from the component to a secondary storage system or via the cloud platform of the vendor. Cegeka will restore the configuration in case of failure based on the last known situation. Software lifecycle management: Cegeka will notify Customer of new software releases relevant to maintain the agreed service level. Cegeka will perform an update of the Managed SD-WAN Security gateway and central management and analytics components based on planned work schedule with a minimum of 1 release per year. Security Patch management: Adjacent to software lifecycle management, Cegeka will engage in emergency patch management for critical software vulnerabilities with a high risk of exploitation within the Managed SD-WAN architecture. Service Desk: The Service Desk is a single point of contact for Managed SD-WAN service inquiries. It provides information, manages requests, and triggers changes or escalations to ensure timely resolution. Availability management - Full Pro-Active Monitoring: Surveillance of the solution components in real-time to identify abnormal behaviour of any involved components. Pro Active Monitoring triggers the process of Incident management. Event management, Incident management and Problem management: Processes are designed to react to proactive monitoring triggers. Unplanned events or a reduction in service quality are managed in accordance with the established SLAs. Root cause analysis is conducted to prevent the recurrence of incidents. On-site repair / express part replacement: As part of the incident management process, if an incident suggests a potential defective hardware, an onsite intervention is planned in accordance with the contract and the specified intervention window and repair time. The service level is defined per site. Service Level Management: service metrics are made available via the Horizon platform. The service of a Service level Manager for the Managed SD-WAN is optional for the service. Change management Standard changes: standard changes are listed in the service request summary. A standard change is pre-approved, with well-defined alteration and with established procedures for implementation. Cegeka can still refuse to implement a standard change request, in this case a motivation will be provided.

Service Request Summary.content: Requests related to this Service can be ordered and will result in the execution of that request by means of a Cegeka change order. Each request has a throughput time to deliver the request (after approval or pre-approval on the customer’s CAB). The SLA for implementing standard changes requested by the customer is 2 business days or otherwise as specified in the change by using the ‘need by date’. LisT OF standard changes Rcode Name of the Change Definition R000199 Manage subnets Modify directly or indirectly connected network segments R000198 Manage static routes Static routes are used to reach subnets that are behind a layer 3 switch or otherwise not directly connected on the appliance R000188 Manage DHCP settings Lease time, DNS servers, Boot options, Boot next-server, Boot filename, DHCP options, Reserved IP ranges, Fixed IP assignments R000194 Manage network propagation in SD-WAN Choose which local networks to export over VPN R000190 Manage internet uplink selection flow preferences Define traffic assigned to a designated uplink based on TCP, UDP, Any – Source & Destination IP and ports R000197 Manage SD-WAN uplink selection flow preferences Implement custom expressions to determine uplink selection R000189 Manage global bandwidth limit per client Apply bandwidth restrictions per client globally R000200 Manage traffic shaping rules Implement advanced traffic shaping policies with multiple traffic shaping rules R000196 Manage SD-WAN VPN outbound firewall rules Configure outbound firewall rules for VPN traffic R000201 Manage uplink bandwidth Adjust uplink bandwidth settings R000187 Manage custom performance class Define custom performance classes for SD-WAN R000192 Manage layer 3 outbound firewall rules Based on protocol, source and destinations, source port and destination ports R000193 Manage layer 7 outbound firewall rules Based on applications R000202 Manage URL allowlisting-denylisting Add or remove URLs/domains from allowlists or denylists R000191 Manage IP denylisting Add or remove an IP range to the denylist R000195 Manage public address translations Modify NAT or PAT rules to facilitate communication between internal and external networks and accommodate new services or servers  Non-standard changes Any changes with an impact on the monthly recurring fee are considered as a non-standard change Any change that does not appear in the service request summary is regarded as a non-standard Any non-standard change must be considered as a project and is evaluated by a Presales and Architecture team. Non-standard changes are billed in a T&M or according to a specific agreement.

Out of scope.content: Everything that is not explicitly mentioned in this service description is by default out of scope.

Service Levels Management.content: The standard service level provided with the product is 8x5 support with a 6-hour Time to Repair (TTR) . It is possible to adapt both the service window and the TTR based on the chosen design and specific operational requirements. The SLA is defined per site and depends on the selected design.  The Service Level Agreement, based on a service window, are composed of: Service Availability availability of the Managed SD-WAN The service is considered available if the equipment or set of equipment’s required to provide the service is visible in the central management tool. The service is considered available if it is possible to send traffic via the connected WAN interface(s) in accordance with the chosen topology (Single Device/Single Circuit, Single Device/Single Circuit and Mobile Backup, Single Device/Dual Circuits, Single Device/Dual Circuits and Mobile Backup, Dual Devices/Dual Circuits). The traffic may be traffic to another SD-WAN site, or internet breakout traffic. In the case of a Dual Devices/Dual Circuits, providing service on backup equipment does not constitute unavailability. Temporary service degradation is not considered service unavailability. Time to Repair TTR refers to the amount of time it takes to resolve an incident with a PX priority. TTR for all type of P are calculated but only P1 incidents are used to perform the availability calculation. The calculation starts when an incident is created and a priority associated; time is running until the resolution of the incident and the return into a fully operational state of the service. Stop clock mechanism may be used according to exclusion list. Incident Notification time When a ticket is opened by Cegeka or the customer, an Incident notification according to the severity of the incident is issued. The notification is pushed in Horizon or via other agreed mechanisms. The incident notification is measured with the associated ticket. SLAs are enabled by the Proactive Monitoring environment. The goal of Proactive Monitoring is to continuously monitor and communicate about the health of the technology used within the managed service. Cegeka is responsible for the following activities regarding Proactive Monitoring: Pro-active Monitor the health and availability the Managed Services components Generating incidents through the monitoring when action(s) are required. Notify the customer via Horizon or other commonly agreed mechanism of the ticket creation according to the service SLAs. Resolve incident(s) based upon the standard service SLAs provided in the service description (P1, P2, P3, P4 TTR) Being easily reachable to help customer for specific requests.

Proactive Monitoring - Incident Prioriti.content: The priority of an incident is based on the impact and urgency . Definitions of impact and urgency Classification Impact Urgency High Medium Low Impact-Urgency Matrix Urgency Impact High Medium Low High Prio 1 Prio 2 Prio 3 Medium Prio 2 Prio 3 Prio 4 Low Prio 3 Prio 4 Prio 4 Definitions of priority Priority Classification Description 1 Critical The incident has caused a complete and immediate work stoppage affecting a primary business process or a broad group of users such as an entire department, floor, branch, line of business, or external Customer. No work around available. 2 High A business process affected in such a way that business functions are severely degraded, multiple users are impacted, or key personnel is affected. A workaround can be made available; however not easily sustainable. 3 Normal A business process is affected in such a way that certain functions are unavailable to end users, or a system and/or service is degraded. A workaround can be available. 4 Low An incident that has little impact on normal business processes and can be handled on a scheduled basis.

P3, P4 are notified but not considered for calculation. Notification can be via Horizon, mail or other type of solutions  Service Availability SLA SD-WAN Topology: Single Device, Dual Circuits Upgrade Description Service availability SLA Single CPE, Dual Accesses  Exclusion for availability and TTR Cegeka Scheduled maintenance Customer initiated maintenance or change request, Customer issue (cabling, power, …) Underlay provider issue or scheduled maintenance Force Majeure Assumptions Service demarcation points are - the Ethernet port(s) of the SD-WAN CPE connecting the Underlay provider(s) Equipment(s) - the Ethernet port of the SD-WAN CPE connecting the customer internal network Interval of measurement 1 min Frequency of reporting Monthly Source and method SD-WAN Central management platform Calculation Device Availability (%) =    100 * (total minutes per month – Site Outage Minutes P1) / total minutes per month Service Window 8x5 12x5, 12x6 Objective (SLA) 99.5% yearly  SLA Time To Repair (TTR) for SD-WAN Upgrade Description Time to Repair refers to the amount of time it takes to resolve an incident with a Px priority.  Service Window 8x5 (same as the Service Availability SLA) Incident Notification for P1, P2 15 Min P1 Time to Repair 6h 4h P2 Time to Repair 8h  P3 Time to Repair 16h (2wd)  P4 Time to Repair N/A Informative only  Calculation TTR The TTR is calculated when an incident is logged as a ticket, associated to a PX by Cegeka via automation or manual action until the restoration of Service in an operational state. The customer is also allowed to create ticket and ask for specific Px. Automation rule is prime vs customer requirement. Calculation is per incident  Calculation Incident Notification A P1 or a P2 incident needs to be notified to the customer within the agreed timeframe. P3, P4 are notified but not considered for calculation. Notification can be via Horizon, mail or other type of solutions  Service Availability SLA SD-WAN Topology: Single Device, Dual Circuits, Mobile Backup Upgrade Description Service availability SLA Single CPE, Dual Accesses, Mobile Backup  Exclusion for availability and TTR Cegeka Scheduled maintenance Customer initiated maintenance or change request, Customer issue (cabling, power, …) Underlay provider issue or scheduled maintenance Force Majeure Assumptions Service demarcation points are - the Ethernet port(s) of the SD-WAN CPE connecting the Underlay provider(s) Equipment(s) - the Ethernet port of the SD-WAN CPE connecting the customer internal network When a mobile gateway is used, the cellular wireless signal of the LTE provider has a - RSRQ Value range in dBM-7 > RSRQ >= -10 - RSRP Value range in dBM 83 > RSRP >= -92 Interval of measurement 1 min Frequency of reporting Monthly Source and method SD-WAN Central management platform Calculation Device Availability (%) =    100 * (total minutes per month – Site Outage Minutes P1) / total minutes per month Service Window 8x5 12x5, 12x6 Objective (SLA) 99.9% yearly  SLA Time To Repair (TTR) for SD-WAN Upgrade Description Time to Repair refers to the amount of time it takes to resolve an incident with a Px priority.  Service Window 8x5 (same as the Service Availability SLA) Incident Notification for P1, P2 15 Min P1 Time to Repair 6h 4h P2 Time to Repair 8h  P3 Time to Repair 16h (2wd)  P4 Time to Repair N/A Informative only  Calculation TTR The TTR is calculated when an incident is logged as a ticket, associated to a PX by Cegeka via automation or manual action until the restoration of Service in an operational state. The customer is also allowed to create ticket and ask for specific Px. Automation rule is prime vs customer requirement. Calculation is per incident  Calculation Incident Notification A P1 or a P2 incident needs to be notified to the customer within the agreed timeframe. P3, P4 are notified but not considered for calculation. Notification can be via Horizon, mail or other type of solutions  Service Availability SLA SD-WAN Topology type: Dual Devices, Dual Circuits Upgrade Description Service availability SLA Dual CPEs, Dual Accesses  Exclusion for availability and TTR Cegeka Scheduled maintenance Customer initiated maintenance or change request, Customer issue (cabling, power, …) Underlay provider issue or scheduled maintenance Force Majeure Assumptions Service demarcation points are - the Ethernet ports of the SD-WAN CPE’s and/or Cegeka Managed Switch(es) connecting the Underlay provider(s) Equipment(s) - the Ethernet ports of the SD-WAN CPE’s connecting the customer internal network Interval of measurement 1 min Frequency of reporting Monthly Source and method SD-WAN Central management platform Calculation Device Availability (%) =    100 * (total minutes per month – Site Outage Minutes P1) / total minutes per month Service Window 8x5 12x5, 12x6,24x7 Objective (SLA) 99.99% yearly  SLA Time To Repair (TTR) for SD-WAN Upgrade Description Time to Repair refers to the amount of time it takes to resolve an incident with a Px priority. The customer is also allowed to create ticket and ask for specific Px. Automation rule is prime vs customer requirement. Notification can be via Horizon, mail or other type of solutions

Service Prerequisites & Conditions.content: This section describes the conditions that need to be met so that Cegeka can provide the described Service .

SD-WAN Security Gateway (Physical/Hardwa.content: The SD-WAN Security Gateway Hardware devices and/or virtual machines (Meraki MX or vMX) required for the Managed SD-WAN service, including all associated costs, are ordered by Cegeka on behalf of the customer as part of the service delivery. These costs are fully charged to the customer, and ownership of the hardware and/or virtual machines is transferred to the customer at the time of purchase. Cegeka is responsible for maintaining operational compliance and managing the hardware and VM environment during the agreed contract term, within the limits of the described service and terms in the Service Description. The customer, as the legal owner of the hardware and virtual machines, delegates operational control to Cegeka for the duration of the contract. Cegeka will manage day-to-day operations, monitor usage, and notify the customer of any required actions, strictly within the limits and scope defined in the present Service Description. If a renewal or replacement of any hardware or VM resource is required during the contract duration (e.g., due to end-of-life, capacity upgrade requirements, or failure outside warranty), the customer, as the legal owner, is responsible for executing and financing the renewal or replacement to ensure uninterrupted service and compliance.

Licenses and/or subscriptions Managed SD.content: Cisco Meraki Licenses and/or subscriptions, including their associated costs, are provided as part of the service cost and transferred to the customer at the time of purchase. Cegeka is responsible for maintaining license and/or subscription compliance during the agreed contract term and will proactively inform the customer about any compliance requirements or actions needed. The customer, as the legal owner of the licenses and subscriptions, delegates license and subscription control to Cegeka for the duration of the contract. Cegeka will manage compliance, monitor usage, and notify the customer of any required actions, strictly within the limits and scope defined in the present Service Description. Renewal Obligation If a renewal of any license or subscription is required during the contract duration, the customer, as the legal owner, is responsible for executing and financing the renewal to ensure uninterrupted service and compliance.

Customer owned environment licenses and/.header: Customer owned environment licenses and/or subscriptions
Customer owned environment licenses and/.content: For environments owned and managed by the customer, such as public cloud platforms (e.g. Azure, AWS, GCP) or private cloud infrastructure , the customer is fully responsible for obtaining, maintaining, and renewing all licenses and/or subscriptions required for the setup and operation of these environments. It is assumed that these licenses and/or subscriptions are valid and sufficient to host the Cegeka SD-WAN managed services. Cegeka will not provide or finance these licenses but may offer advisory support on compliance requirements when relevant E.g. Advising on the correct license tier for hosting Cegeka Managed SD-WAN services (e.g., ensuring an Azure subscription includes required compute and networking features).

Contract Duration.content: The service is provided with a minimum contract duration of one year.

Terms and Conditions.content: Refers to “CEGEKA TERMS AND CONDITIONS FOR SECURITY SERVICES”

Technical Implementation Pre-Requisites.content: Performance specifications Performance specifications are as provided by the vendor. All performance values are “up to” and vary depending on system configuration and feature usage. Cegeka is not responsible if the published performance values are not achieved, as actual results may differ based on design choices, deployment conditions, and operational factors Activities Standard Activities are performed during Belgian business hours and business days using business hours rate basis. Business hours rate basis is between 9:00 and 18:00 hours on business days. Business days are from Monday to Friday, excluding Saturdays, Sundays and public holidays. On-site installation and on-site repair On-site installation and on-site repair are only available if referenced in the list of supported countries in the annex document “Cegeka Managed SD-WAN Service availability Matrix” Self-service installation and self-service repair are available worldwide. Construction works Any construction works on private domain are not included in the agreement pricing. Shipping costs Shipping of equipment(s) is not included as a standard service. Cabling Any cabling (Structured and non-structured), except for patch cabling, are not included in the standard service. Cellular gateway Cabling and installation of the cellular gateway (Meraki MG) is always quoted in a specific annex after a site check (via phone or on premise). SIM card The SIM card (subscriber identity module) and subscription plan are out of scope of this service. Electrical outlets Enough power outlets are available to support the devices required to provide the Managed SD-WAN service Power capacity The circuit can handle the load to support the device(s). Grounding and Bonding Proper grounding and bonding to prevent electrical surges and static discharge. Temperature & humidity The location should maintain appropriate temperature and humidity levels as specified by the device manufacturer to prevent overheating and moisture damage Ventilation Adequate airflow is necessary to dissipate heat. Dust and Cleanliness The area should be free from excessive dust and contaminants that can affect device performance. Size and Space Sufficient physical space to accommodate the device(s), including any necessary racks, shelves and/or mounting equipment. Accessibility Easy access for maintenance and troubleshooting, with enough space around the device(s) for ventilation and cable management. Structural Support For heavy devices, ensure the structural integrity of the mounting location (e.g., walls, ceilings). Cabling Proper Ethernet cables (Cat6, etc.) and fiber optics are used with cable management systems to organize and protect cables. Ports and Switches Availability of network ports or switches to connect the device(s) to the existing network infrastructure with the appropriate speeds. Signal Strength For wireless or radio devices, ensure strong and consistent signal strength. Identification (Underlay) All necessary underlay uplink ports are made available and clearly identified and labelled as “WAN” or “Internet”. Connectivity (Underlay) Ensure that the connection is active and functional prior to the installation of the SD-WAN Security gateway device or equipment’s required to deploy the Managed SD-WAN service. Physical Security Secure location to prevent unauthorized access, such as locked rooms or enclosures. Compliance and Regulations Compliance with local building codes and regulations, including fire safety and electrical standards. (power, protection, lighting, safe access, environmental requirements, ). Building permits Where applicable, the customer is responsible for applying for and obtaining all building permits required for the installation of equipment needed to provide the service. Cegeka will not be held responsible for any failure to do so, as the installation is carried out under the customer's authority.
